mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in the unemployment rate reduce household income and consumer spending, leading to downward earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months when UNRATE is rising, or Rank IC is above 0 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 2.5% signals emergency monetary easing during acute financial stress; this level confirms systemic impairment of credit intermediation, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month as risk premiums expand.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS is below 2.5%, or the conditional mean return fails to underperform the unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous negative momentum in industrial production and rising unemployment create a feedback loop where output contraction and labor market deterioration jointly amplify earnings decline, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction between INDPRO and UNRATE changes does not significantly predict 60-day returns (p > 0.05), or the signal Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a housing-led recession, consecutive monthly declines in housing starts signal accelerating weakness in residential construction and durable goods demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative following two consecutive HOUST declines, or the hit rate is below 45% during housing-led downturns.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly declines in industrial production trigger immediate de-risking by institutional investors anticipating near-term earnings misses, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when INDPRO declines month-over-month, or the mean conditional return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter contraction in real GDP causes a sustained repricing of cyclical earnings and equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns following negative GDP growth quarters are positive more than 50% of the time, or the conditional mean return is not significantly below the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with falling industrial production creates a stagflationary profit margin squeeze; higher input costs and lower output volumes jointly compress earnings, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint effect of CPIAUCSL increases and INDPRO decreases does not significantly predict negative 20-day returns (interaction t-stat > -1.5), or Rank IC is above -0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales reflect strong consumer demand that translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following monthly RSAFS increases are negative more than 45% of the time, or the Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.
